title: The Prime Number Theorem: zero-free regions, equidistribution, and the modern frontier — Proof DAG
description: The dependency structure of prime distribution, anchored on the Prime Number Theorem. The classical spine — Riemann's explicit formula → a zero-free region for ζ → PNT with error term → primes in arithmetic progressions (Dirichlet, Siegel–Walfisz, Bombieri–Vinogradov) — is pre-arXiv and lives in `foundational`; it is the layer with a Lean blueprint (the PNT+ project), so it doubles as the external ground truth for validating extracted nodes and edges (see `blueprint_anchor`). The arXiv-era spine carries the program forward in three directions: (1) effective/explicit PNT — sharpened and fully-explicit zero-free regions (Ford; Mossinghoff–Trudgian) feeding an explicit error term (Platt–Trudgian); (2) equidistribution beyond Bombieri–Vinogradov — Maynard's primes-in-APs-to-large-moduli trilogy and the nilsequence BV theorem; (3) short intervals — the Matomäki–Radziwiłł multiplicative-functions method. This DAG is the keystone connecting the two existing number-theory DAGs: it shares the zeta-zeros machinery with `large-values-zero-density` (zero-free regions vs. zero-density estimates are the two ways zeros control primes), and its equidistribution layer is the level-of-distribution input the Selberg/Maynard sieve in `maynard-small-gaps` consumes — a genuine theorem-level `provides_input_to` link, not a thematic one.

1501.04585.title: Multiplicative functions in short intervals
1501.04585.role: short-intervals engine — multiplicative functions have small variance in intervals [x, x+h] with h→∞; the technical heart behind modern short-interval prime and Chowla-type results

2007.04290.title: Multiplicative functions in short intervals II
2007.04290.role: extends the short-intervals method (II) — sharper ranges and the averaged / almost-all setting

2006.08250.title: Primes in arithmetic progressions to large moduli III: Uniform residue classes
2006.08250.role: uniform residue classes (III) — uniformity over residue classes; the level-of-distribution input most directly relevant to bounded-gaps sieve applications. Parts I (2006.06572) and II (2006.07088) of the large-moduli trilogy are extracted in the maynard-small-gaps DAG (see `related_dags`) and reached from here via cross-DAG edges in graph.json — not duplicated as files in this DAG

2006.05954.title: The Bombieri-Vinogradov theorem for nilsequences
2006.05954.role: higher-order BV — equidistribution of primes against nilsequences; the Gowers-norm / higher-order-Fourier extension of Bombieri–Vinogradov

1910.08205.title: Zero-free regions for the Riemann zeta function
1910.08205.role: modern zero-free region — sharpened Vinogradov–Korobov-type zero-free region for ζ; the qualitative input to the classical PNT error term

2212.06867.title: Explicit zero-free regions for the Riemann zeta-function
2212.06867.role: explicit zero-free region — fully explicit constants; the effective input that explicit PNT bounds consume

1809.03134.title: The error term in the prime number theorem
1809.03134.role: explicit PNT error term — fully explicit bound on |ψ(x)−x|, consuming the explicit zero-free region; the computational/effective PNT frontier and the natural quantitative target the PNT+ blueprint aims at

related_dags.large-values-zero-density: Two sides of how zeros of ζ control primes. This DAG carries the zero-FREE-region side (no zeros near Re(s)=1 ⟹ classical PNT error term); that DAG carries the zero-DENSITY side (few zeros off the critical line ⟹ primes in short intervals). Shared zeta machinery and shared frontier authors (Maynard, Tao). The Guth–Maynard (2405.20552) and Tao–Trudgian–Yang (2501.16779) zero-density results there are the density-side complement to the zero-free-region + error-term spine here; no node is duplicated, the link is a cross-DAG `addresses_same_question` / `shared_mechanism` between the zero-free-region nodes here and the A(σ) nodes there.
related_dags.maynard-small-gaps: `provides_input_to` — load-bearing, not thematic, and partly realised through shared nodes. Maynard's large-moduli I & II (2006.06572, 2006.07088) already live as nodes in that DAG and are shared here; large-moduli III (2006.08250) is added by this DAG. Together with Bombieri–Vinogradov and the Elliott–Halberstam conjecture (foundational), these are the level-of-distribution input the Selberg/Maynard sieve in that DAG consumes — the sieve cannot run without a level of distribution, and these papers are exactly what establish it. Polymath8a's MPZ equidistribution estimates already in that DAG are the same frontier reached by a different route, so the cross-DAG edge connects the large-moduli / well-factorable nodes to the MPZ and DHL-reduction nodes (e.g. Maynard 1311.4600v3 prop:4.1) there.

blueprint_anchor.role: External ground truth for the classical-core layer. PNT+ formalizes the Prime Number Theorem (Newman's Tauberian proof and the Wiener–Ikehara route), the Prime Number Theorem in arithmetic progressions, and the classical error term, with the Chebotarev density theorem as a stretch goal. Its formalized statement-level dependency graph gives a precision/recall yardstick for our extracted nodes and edges on the PNT spine — the same validation role the PFR Lean blueprint plays for additive combinatorics. The contrast is the point: the blueprint maps the inside of the classical proof; this DAG extends outward to the effective-PNT, equidistribution, and short-interval literature the blueprint does not reach.

Riemann:1859.title: Ueber die Anzahl der Primzahlen unter einer gegebenen Grösse
Riemann:1859.role: foundational — the explicit formula linking ψ(x) to the zeros of ζ; the root of the whole program

Hadamard:1896.title: Sur la distribution des zéros de la fonction ζ(s) et ses conséquences arithmétiques
Hadamard:1896.role: foundational — Prime Number Theorem (with de la Vallée Poussin), via no zeros on Re(s)=1

delaValleePoussin:1896.title: Recherches analytiques sur la théorie des nombres premiers
delaValleePoussin:1896.role: foundational — PNT and the first classical zero-free region + error term

Dirichlet:1837.title: Beweis des Satzes, dass jede unbegrenzte arithmetische Progression ... unendlich viele Primzahlen enthält
Dirichlet:1837.role: foundational — primes in arithmetic progressions; L-functions

SiegelWalfisz:1936.title: Siegel–Walfisz theorem (Siegel 1935; Walfisz 1936)
SiegelWalfisz:1936.role: foundational — PNT in APs uniform for small moduli (ineffective, via Siegel's bound on exceptional zeros)

Linnik:1944.title: On the least prime in an arithmetic progression
Linnik:1944.role: foundational — Linnik's theorem; the log-free zero-density and exceptional-zero repulsion machinery

BombieriVinogradov:1965.title: The Bombieri–Vinogradov theorem (Bombieri 1965; A. I. Vinogradov 1965)
BombieriVinogradov:1965.role: foundational — primes in APs on average to level 1/2 (GRH on average); the level-of-distribution that downstream sieves consume

ElliottHalberstam:1968.title: A conjecture in prime number theory
ElliottHalberstam:1968.role: foundational — the EH conjecture (level of distribution arbitrarily close to 1); the conditional input that sharpens bounded-gaps results

Newman:1980.title: Simple analytic proof of the prime number theorem
Newman:1980.role: foundational — the short Tauberian proof of PNT; this is the route the PNT+ Lean blueprint formalizes

Korevaar:1982.title: On Newman's quick way to the prime number theorem
Korevaar:1982.role: foundational — the standard exposition of Newman's contour argument used in formalizations
